creator: editor@callfortesting.org
text:
pkg info| grep samba
samba419-4.19.5_1         Free SMB/CIFS and AD/DC server and client for Unix

Here is what I am seeing:

samba_dnsupdate --verbose --all-names
No such file or directory: '/usr/bin/nsupdate'

pkg install samba-nsupdate
...
samba-nsupdate-9.16.5_1   nsupdate utility with the GSS-TSIG support

which samba_dnsupdate
/usr/local/sbin/samba_dnsupdate

samba_dnsupdate --verbose --all-names

Successfully obtained Kerberos ticket to DNS/win-928ubummcm2.mydomain.mycompany.local as FREEBSD$
Failed nsupdate: CNAME 6fc070c2-f232-48ec-9289-e4e72d9910bd._msdcs.mydomain.mycompany.local freebsd.mydomain.mycompany.local : [Errno 2] No such file or directory: '/usr/bin/nsupdate'

Being a test system:

ln -s /usr/local/sbin/samba_dnsupdate /usr/bin/nsupdate

samba_dnsupdate --verbose --all-names

Successfully obtained Kerberos ticket to DNS/win-928ubummcm2.mydomain.mycompany.local as FREEBSD$
Usage: samba_dnsupdate [options]

nsupdate: error: no such option: -g
Failed nsupdate: 2

1. /usr/bin/nsupdate sounds wrong, being a port
2. error: no such option: -g
3. Should samba-nsupdate be set as a samba dependency given that is might not be discoverable to some users?

Is there anything else I should try?
